metric_value: ~40% of mainframe users
notes: Aberdeen Q1-2004 survey of 98 mainframe users: approximately 40% open to shifting to Itanium-based platforms; attracted by price/performance and flexibility advantages.

metric_value: 40% looking to offload or surround mainframes
notes: Aberdeen field research: 40% of mainframe customers are now looking at ways to offload or surround their mainframes.

metric_value: Itanium 2 superior to Xeon; comparable or superior to mainframe
notes: Interviewees report: Itanium 2-based platforms offer comparable or superior functionality to mainframes in programmer productivity and manageability; Itanium superior to Xeon in performance and scalability.

metric_value: Xeon-based mainframe solutions already in production 1+ years
notes: Xeon's ability to handle migrated mainframe-class workloads is evident: Xeon solutions for mainframe workloads already operating for one or more years.

metric_value: Straightforward; no code changes required
notes: Interviewees report migrating COBOL, FORTRAN, and DB2-based applications from mainframe to Intel is straightforward — does not require code changes; most code involves only COBOL and DB2.

metric_value: Significant but doable
notes: CICS, DL/1, and IBM assembler code migration to Intel requires significant but doable effort per some interviewees.

metric_value: IBM increasingly discounting mainframe prices
notes: Over past year, IBM has increasingly discounted mainframe prices to maintain strong position with customers as they add applications and increase workloads.

metric_value: Mainframe hardware + software licensing perceived as more expensive than open systems
notes: Aberdeen interviewees indicate mainframe hardware and associated software licensing costs perceived as more expensive than open system architecture alternatives.

metric_value: Largest payback: CPU-intensive workloads (batch, data warehousing, analytics)
notes: CPU-intensive applications (batch, technical/scientific, data warehousing, analytics like SAS) deliver largest immediate payback on Itanium vs. mainframe; nuance: mainframe processing power is expensive, not that Intel I/O is deficient.

metric_value: More scalable, more cost-effective, more flexible, smaller footprint, more programmer-productive
notes: Interviewees report Intel-based platforms: better performing per processor, more scalable, more cost-effective, more flexible (standards-based/open), more programmer-productive, smaller footprint; equally robust and manageable.

metric_value: AMD Opteron seen as less real-world-tested
notes: Interviewees do not view AMD Opteron 64-bit as comparable alternative; seen as less real-world-tested for mainframe-class workloads.

metric_value: Linux on mainframe less manageable and less performant
notes: Linux on the mainframe viewed as less manageable and less performant than Intel-based alternatives.

metric_value: Surround / Offload / Replace / Migrate
notes: Aberdeen defines four mainframe migration strategies: 'Surround' (new workloads on Itanium), 'Offload' (Intel app takes over some mainframe work), 'Replace' (rewrite application), 'Migrate' (port existing code).

metric_value: Itanium will increasingly host former mainframe workloads
notes: Aberdeen predicts: as Itanium becomes more familiar in mainframe shops, existing and new workloads that belonged exclusively on mainframe will increasingly be hosted on Intel Itanium.

metric_value: [UNVERIFIED]
Replace strategy has cultural resistance and long implementation time
notes: Aberdeen: 'Replace' strategies requiring rewrite of mainframe applications take long to implement with significant cultural resistance; users logically prefer 'surround', 'offload', or migration strategies.

metric_value: 98 qualified mainframe users surveyed
notes: Aberdeen Q1-2004 research: in-depth qualitative interviews with U.S., European, and Asian Itanium users for mainframe workloads, plus quantitative survey of 98 qualified mainframe users.

metric_value: Itanium ecosystem matured; nearly all major EBAs support Itanium with customers in production
notes: Itanium 'ecosystem' (core applications, tools, enabling software) has matured; nearly all major enterprise business applications provide Itanium support with customers in production as of Q1-2004.

metric_value: Most success stories just reaching production in Q1-2004
notes: Itanium 2-based solution case studies and references are newly arrived; many success stories just recently coming to production in Q1-2004 — news not previously reported.

metric_value: DB2 on Intel multiprocessor more cost-effective than mainframe VM-based allocation
notes: DB2 application performance using Intel multiprocessor appears more cost-effective than typical mainframe configuration involving allocation of fractions of processors to application in VM environment.
